alignments.rationale: The core contribution claim for DaRL is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Distribution-Aware Sample Augmentation (DASA) module improves the generalizability of LLM-based relevance modeling by selecting appropriate out-of-distribution samples for fine-tuning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DaRL utilizes out-of-distribution (OOD) detection techniques to actively select appropriate samples for model fine-tuning, improving the generalizability of LLM-based relevance modeling. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the mechanism (OOD detection) and presents the claim with higher certainty, strengthening the original medium-certainty claim about sample selection and generalizability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DaRL has been successfully deployed online to serve Alipay's insurance product search. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both offline experiments on real-world industry data and online A/B testing demonstrate that DaRL effectively improves the performance of relevance modeling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the deployment claim into concrete evaluation results (offline experiments and online A/B tests) showing effectiveness, broadening the evidence base and amplifying confidence compared with the single deployment statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data-to-Knowledge pipelines contribute efficiency gains and industrial scalability in a World Wide Lab. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation demonstrates that semantically enriched, cross-organizational D2K pipelines can accelerate model development and reduce redundant data collection within a constrained but practically relevant class of robotics tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication supports the general efficiency/scalability claim but narrows it to a constrained class of robotics tasks and clarifies the specific benefits (accelerated development, reduced redundant data).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed concept builds on a network of Digital Shadows, augmenting the Digital Twins concept. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation demonstrates that semantically enriched, cross-organizational D2K pipelines can accelerate model development and reduce redundant data collection within a constrained but practically relevant class of robotics tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate the 'Digital Shadows' framing but demonstrates the implemented concept's benefits; this is a looser correspondence and thus lower confidence and framed as a clarification/amplification of the conceptual claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proof of concept captures and semantically annotates trajectory data from multiple similar but independent robots in a data lakehouse. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Data-to-Knowledge (D2K) pipeline captures and semantically annotates robotic trajectory data from three independent research institutes to train an inverse-dynamics foundation model for robot control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the same proof-of-concept claim with more specific detail (three institutes) and explicit downstream use (training inverse-dynamics model), clarifying the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An independent process dynamically queries matching data for training an inverse dynamic foundation model for robotic control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Data-to-Knowledge (D2K) pipeline captures and semantically annotates robotic trajectory data from three independent research institutes to train an inverse-dynamics foundation model for robot control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication consolidates and clarifies the preprint's description of the data-querying/training pipeline by specifying institutions involved and the exact downstream training goal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper proposes three quantum-secure design principles for neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The chapter proposes three principles for quantum-secure design of neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (three principles) with equivalent wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These design principles include using post-quantum cryptography and employing quantum-resistant neural network architectures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed principles include the use of post-quantum cryptography and quantum-resistant neural network architectures.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same specific principles (post-quantum cryptography and quantum-resistant architectures) with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed design principles aim to ensure the integrity and reliability of machine learning models in the quantum era.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The chapter proposes three principles for quantum-secure design of neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the proposed principles themselves but does not explicitly restate the stated aim; this maps to the same contribution with somewhat less explicit emphasis on 'integrity and reliability.'

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper identifies open issues in quantum security, privacy, and trust.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The chapter identifies open issues in quantum security, privacy, and trust.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same limitation/identification of open issues verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future directions include exploring adaptive adversarial attacks and auto adversarial attacks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recommendations include exploring adaptive adversarial attacks and auto-adversarial attacks as future directions.
alignments.rationale: The publication lists the same future directions with equivalent phrasing and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LogBabylon is a centralized log data consolidating solution that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) integrated with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) technology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LogBabylon is a centralized log data consolidating solution that leverages Large Language Models integrated with Retrieval-Augmented Generation technology.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the identical contribution description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LogBabylon interprets the log data in a human-readable way and adds insight analysis of the system performance and anomaly alerts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LogBabylon interprets the log data in a human-readable way and adds insight analysis of the system performance and anomaly alerts.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is carried forward verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LogBabylon significantly reduces the time and effort required to interpret complex datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LogBabylon significantly reduces the time and effort required to interpret complex datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same performance/efficiency claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LogBabylon enhances the accuracy and relevancy of the extracted information from diverse log sources.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LogBabylon enhances the accuracy and relevancy of the extracted information from diverse log sources.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this result claim with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LogBabylon supports more effective decision-making and operational efficiency through a deeper understanding of log data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LogBabylon supports more effective decision-making and operational efficiency through a deeper understanding of log data.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same concluding claim about decision-making and efficiency.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advancements in AR hardware and AI capabilities are making everyday AR increasingly feasible.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same high-level conclusion verbatim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The workshop will explore how AI can drive everyday AR experiences.
alignments.rationale: The contribution description of the workshop is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The workshop aims to identify opportunities and challenges in AI-enabled AR.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The workshop aims to identify opportunities and challenges in AI-enabled AR.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the workshop aims without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The goal of the workshop is to create novel AR experiences that seamlessly blend the digital and physical worlds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The goal of the workshop is to create novel AR experiences that seamlessly blend the digital and physical worlds.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the workshop goal identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves a performance exceeding 94% for detection and classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves about 92% success in detection and classification when using DAS data only.
alignments.rationale: The published result reports a lower numeric performance and explicitly limits the result to DAS-only data, reducing and clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model has a false alarm rate of about 1.2%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model has a false alarm rate of about 3.6% when compared to YOLO outputs.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a higher false alarm rate and situates the metric relative to YOLO outputs, changing the numeric value and clarifying the comparison context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach highlights the potential of combining fiber-optic sensors with visual information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach highlights the potential of combining fiber-optic sensors and cameras for traffic monitoring.
alignments.rationale: The published claim narrows the general idea to a specific application (traffic monitoring) and specifies 'cameras', refining the original, more general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of DAS data with visual information could benefit future smart city developments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of DAS data with calibrated video recordings provides statistical insights that could benefit future smart city developments.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds detail about calibrated video recordings and 'statistical insights', making the claim more specific while retaining the same general implication for smart cities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset shared encourages future research in the field.
alignments.rationale: There is no corresponding claim in the publication about a shared dataset encouraging future research, indicating this preprint claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed dual deep learning strategy improves model performance for license plate detection and recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed dual deep learning strategy improves model performance for license plate detection and recognition.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same contribution statement without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves a recall rate of 92% on the CENPARMI dataset and 90% on the UFPR-ALPR dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves a recall rate of 92% on the CENPARMI dataset and 90% on the UFPR-ALPR dataset.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant performance discrepancies in license plate recognition are influenced by font characteristics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant performance discrepancies in license plate recognition are influenced by font characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about font characteristics affecting performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current methods for license plate detection often require tailoring to specific regional datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current methods for license plate detection often require tailoring to specific regional datasets.
alignments.rationale: The limitation regarding regional tailoring is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bias is a natural phenomenon that influences both human behavior and the design of socially expressive robots.
alignments.rationale: None of the publication claims discuss bias in robot design; they focus on regulation, human-likeness, and interdisciplinary moral/legal issues, so this bias-centric claim was not carried into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recognizing the embedding of bias in robot design is crucial for understanding its implications for society.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not mention embedding of bias or its societal implications, instead addressing legal/policy and human-likeness concerns, indicating this claim was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The chapter discusses two approaches to human-robot interaction: designing robots with bias awareness and using robots to address human bias.
alignments.rationale: Publication claims do not describe these two bias-related HRI approaches; the focus shifted to regulation and ethical/legal challenges, so the specific contribution was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A curated selection of cases from recent HRI research is presented to illustrate the impact of bias in robot design and interaction.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present or mention curated HRI cases about bias; the related preprint contribution does not appear in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The author proposes critical next steps to address challenges and opportunities related to bias in HRI research and practice.
alignments.rationale: Publication claims emphasize policy, regulation, and interdisciplinary framing rather than specific next steps for addressing bias in HRI, so this proposed agenda was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DDPM consistently generated more realistic images with lower FID scores compared to PGGANs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DDPM consistently produced images of higher realism, backed by lower FID scores, and overtakes PGGANs in augmenting classification outcomes of all investigated models and datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the realism/FID result and adds broader performance claims (overtaking PGGANs across models/datasets), which clarifies and broadens the original claim and strengthens confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating DDPM-generated images into the original datasets increased accuracy by up to 6%, enhancing model robustness and stability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The addition of DDPM-generated images to original datasets obtained an improvement of about 6% in accuracy and therefore enhanced robustness and reliability of models, specifically when datasets are unbalanced.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the ~6% accuracy improvement and robustness claim but specifies it applies particularly to unbalanced datasets, narrowing the scope while keeping the claim and confidence intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random Sampling demonstrated superior stability, while Greedy K Sampling offered diversity at the cost of higher FID scores.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim mentions the comparative behavior of Random Sampling vs. Greedy K Sampling, so this specific methodological result was omitted from the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study highlights the efficacy of DDPM in augmenting small, imbalanced medical image datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research identifies the potential of DDPM to effectively augment and balance sparse datasets of medical images and subsequently improve training of models and predictive outcomes.
alignments.rationale: Both texts convey the same conclusion about DDPM's potential to augment/balance sparse or imbalanced medical image datasets and improve model training/outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Caregiver involvement can enhance student academic outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Caregiver involvement can enhance student academic outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about caregiver involvement improving academic outcomes without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Conversational support is a promising method of providing caregivers with the guidance needed to effectively support student learning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Llama 3 LLM generated message recommendations for caregivers supporting their child's math practice via chat.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Llama 3 LLM generated message recommendations for caregivers supporting their child’s math practice via chat.
alignments.rationale: The result about Llama 3 generating message recommendations is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recommendations facilitating content-level support and student metacognition through self-explanation were valued by caregivers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recommendations facilitating content-level support and student metacognition through self-explanation were valued by caregivers.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same finding regarding caregiver valuation of content-level and metacognitive recommendations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Insights into how tutoring systems can best be merged with LLMs to support hybrid tutoring settings were contributed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Insights into how tutoring systems can best be merged with LLMs to support hybrid tutoring settings were contributed.
alignments.rationale: The contribution about merging tutoring systems with LLMs is preserved without change in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improved models for the granular detection and sub-classification of news media bias in English news articles have been developed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Improved models for the granular detection and sub-classification of news media bias in English news articles have been developed.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution about improved granular bias-detection models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of zero-shot models is compared to fine-tuned large pre-trained neural transformer language models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of zero-shot models is compared to fine-tuned large pre-trained neural transformer language models.
alignments.rationale: The comparison between zero-shot and fine-tuned transformer models is carried forward unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The level of detail of the classes affects performance on a novel taxonomy of 27 news bias-types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The level of detail of the classes affects performance on a novel taxonomy of 27 news bias-types.
alignments.rationale: The claim about class granularity affecting performance on the 27-type taxonomy remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using synthetically generated example data can improve the quality of the models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using synthetically generated example data can improve the quality of the models.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the finding that synthetic examples can improve model quality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method incorporates a secondary length-difference positional encoding (LDPE) into the input embeddings to control response length.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method incorporates a secondary length-difference positional encoding (LDPE) into the input embeddings for precise control of response length in LLMs.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological contribution about LDPE for length control with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fine-tuning with LDPE allows the model to learn to terminate responses coherently at the desired length, achieving mean token errors of less than 3 tokens.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning with LDPE allows the model to learn to terminate responses coherently at the desired length, achieving mean token errors of less than 3 tokens.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative result about mean token error <3 and coherent termination is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Max New Tokens++ enables flexible upper-bound length control rather than an exact target.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Max New Tokens++ enables flexible upper-bound length control in LLMs.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same claim about flexible upper-bound length control (the wording is slightly more concise but the meaning is unchanged).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that our method enables precise length control without compromising response quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method enables precise length control without compromising response quality.
alignments.rationale: The experimental conclusion about precise length control without quality loss is maintained in the publication with equivalent substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NSGA-II has difficulties with more than two objectives and is very sensitive to the choice of the population size.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this limitation about the classic NSGA-II; the claim about difficulties and sensitivity appears omitted in the published set of claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed tie-breaking rule improves the efficiency of the modified NSGA-II for optimizing the OneMinMax, LeadingOnesTrailingZeros, and OneJumpZeroJump benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modified NSGA-II can optimize the OneMinMax, LeadingOnesTrailingZeros, and OneJumpZeroJump benchmarks efficiently for many objectives.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the efficient optimization result for the modified algorithm but does not explicitly attribute it to the tie-breaking rule, so the specific mechanism was de-emphasized/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modified NSGA-II can optimize the three benchmarks efficiently for many objectives, contrary to the exponential lower runtime bound previously shown for OneMinMax with three or more objectives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modified NSGA-II can optimize the OneMinMax, LeadingOnesTrailingZeros, and OneJumpZeroJump benchmarks efficiently for many objectives.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core positive result about efficient optimization across many objectives but omits the explicit contrast to the prior exponential lower bound, so the statement is presented in a clarified form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For bi-objective problems, the runtime guarantees do not increase when moderately increasing the population size over the minimum admissible size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The runtime guarantees for bi-objective problems do not increase when moderately increasing the population size over the minimum admissible size.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim but expresses it with higher certainty; the content is unchanged while the asserted confidence is stronger.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For population sizes larger than the minimal choice, the modified NSGA-II improves the runtime considerably over the classic NSGA-II.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modified NSGA-II improves considerably over the Θ(N n^k) runtime of the classic NSGA-II for population sizes larger than the minimal choice N = Θ(n).
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same improvement over the classic runtime for larger population sizes, adding a formal expression but preserving the claim content.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The runtime guarantee for the OneJumpZeroJump problem is O(max {n^(k + 1), N n}) function evaluations when the population size is at least four times the size of the Pareto front.
alignments.rationale: This specific, formal runtime bound for OneJumpZeroJump (with the 4x Pareto-front population condition) appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Poor sitting posture can lead to various work-related musculoskeletal disorders (WMSDs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Poor sitting posture can lead to various work-related musculoskeletal disorders (WMSDs).
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same health-risk conclusion verbatim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The ensemble learning model based on the soft voting mechanism achieves the highest F1 score of 98.1%.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The SitPose system was deployed to encourage better sitting posture and to reduce sedentary habits.

alignments.pub_claim_text: SitPose is a sitting posture and sedentary detection system that utilizes the latest Kinect depth camera.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BlockDoor significantly reduces the watermark validation accuracy of the Trigger set by up to 98% without compromising on functionality.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BlockDoor delivers a less than 1% drop on the clean samples while modifying Trigger samples for correct machine learning functionality.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A critical flaw in the design of the watermark verification with backdoors has been identified, related to the behaviour of the samples of the Trigger Set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BlockDoor is a comprehensive package of techniques that blocks three different kinds of Trigger samples used in the literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BlockDoor is a comprehensive package of techniques that blocks all three different kinds of Trigger samples used in the literature.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the certainty of the contribution (higher stated certainty) while preserving the substance that it blocks the three types of triggers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper introduces a competency-based model for generative artificial intelligence literacy that covers essential skills and knowledge areas necessary to interact with generative AI.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article introduces a competency-based model for generative artificial intelligence literacy that covers essential skills and knowledge areas necessary to interact with generative AI.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The 12 competencies provide a framework for individuals, policymakers, government officials, and educators to navigate and take advantage of generative AI responsibly.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Embedding these competencies into educational programs and professional training initiatives can equip individuals to become responsible and informed users and creators of generative AI.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The competencies serve as a roadmap for individuals seeking to become familiar with generative AI and for researchers and policymakers to develop assessments, educational programs, guidelines, and regulations.
alignments.rationale: The publication's wording is slightly adjusted but the claim functionally matches the preprint's roadmap assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural collaborative filtering represents a significant milestone in Recommender Systems by combining matrix factorization and deep neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural collaborative filtering represents a significant milestone in Recommender Systems by combining matrix factorization and deep neural networks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional methods like matrix factorization are limited in their ability to capture complex interactions due to their reliance on linear models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional methods like matrix factorization are limited in their ability to capture complex interactions between users, items, and contexts.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework combines neural contextual matrix factorization with autoencoders to predict user ratings for items.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Insider threats (InTs) within organizations have a disproportionate ability to damage systems, information, and infrastructure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Insider threats (InTs) within organizations have a disproportionate ability to damage systems, information, and infrastructure. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This literature review is the first study to comprehensively organize research across psychological, technical, and educational approaches. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This literature review is the first study to comprehensively organize research on insider threats across psychological, technical, and educational disciplines. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Few training methods prioritize psychological indicators in the context of insider threat detection. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Few training methods prioritize psychological indicators for insider threat detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The substantive content is the same (few methods prioritize psychological indicators); the publication presents it with higher certainty/strength, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The innovative agent-based model can replicate diverse psychological profiles of lost persons and navigate real-world landscapes autonomously. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is restated verbatim in the publication with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The probability distribution map for the potential location of a lost person is generated through Monte Carlo simulations and mobility-time-based sampling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The methodological/result statement is effectively identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Validation of the model using real-world Search and Rescue data demonstrates its effectiveness in predicting the location of lost persons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Validation of the model using real-world Search and Rescue data demonstrates its ability to generalize data for sampling initial starting points. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comparative analysis with historical data indicates that the model shows promising outcomes relative to alternative methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comparative analysis with historical data indicates that the model shows promising outcomes compared to alternative methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative result is restated with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The agent-based model offers adaptability for use in search and rescue operations across various geographical locations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The adaptability claim is substantively the same but presented with increased certainty in the publication, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LightGC$^2$N outperforms nine state-of-the-art methods in accuracy and efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LightGC2N outperforms nine state-of-the-art methods in accuracy and efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AM-SAM eliminates the need for human involvement in generating prompts for an input image, achieving good performance in early training epochs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework eliminates the need for human involvement in early training epochs, thus achieving faster convergence.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes elimination of human involvement specifically in early epochs and adds a claim of faster convergence, which clarifies and strengthens the original claim about early performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mask decoder's feature representation in SAM is sometimes inaccurate due to its reliance on dot product operations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AM-SAM improves feature representation learning by introducing an element-wise multiplication operation that complements the standard dot product.
alignments.rationale: The publication addresses the stated limitation by describing the specific remedy (element-wise multiplication) and thus clarifies how the limitation is mitigated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current solutions to SAM's limitations often require retraining a large number of parameters, which is time-consuming and resource-intensive.
alignments.rationale: This limitation about retraining costs does not appear among the publication claims and so appears omitted in the published set of claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The negative logarithmic likelihood (NLL) generally increases linearly with the number of features, indicating a linear scaling law.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The negative logarithmic likelihood (NLL) generally increases linearly with the number of features M for an untrained generative tensor network (GTN).
alignments.rationale: The publication qualifies the observation by specifying it applies to an untrained GTN, which narrows and clarifies the original general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The linear scaling law is suppressed by a negative quadratic correction as information is gained through training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The linear-scaling law of NLL is suppressed by a negative quadratic correction when gaining information through training.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result with equivalent wording and no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The emergence of the quadratic correction term in NLL for the testing set serves as evidence of the generalization power of the generative tensor network (GTN).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The emergence of a quadratic correction term in the NLL for the testing set is evidence of the generalization power of the GTN.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same conclusion about the quadratic correction indicating generalization power.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Over-parameterization can be identified by the deviation in the values of the quadratic coefficient between training and testing sets while increasing the number of quantum channels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Over-parameterization can be identified by the deviation in the values of α between the training and testing sets while increasing the number of quantum channels χ.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same diagnostic claim but clarifies it using the notation (α, χ), making the statement more explicit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The unveiling of universal scaling laws in quantum-probabilistic machine learning is a valuable step toward establishing a white-box machine learning scheme within the quantum probabilistic framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unveiling universal scaling laws in quantum-probabilistic ML is a valuable step toward establishing a white-box ML scheme within the quantum-probabilistic framework.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this forward-looking contribution claim with equivalent meaning and low certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, Exemplar-guided Image Translation with Brownian-Bridge Diffusion Models (EBDM), formulates the image translation task as a stochastic Brownian bridge process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EBDM formulates the image translation task as a stochastic Brownian bridge process, allowing for translation into photo-realistic images conditioned solely on the given exemplar image.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core methodological formulation; additional result detail in the pub claim is a continuation rather than a substantive change to this contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EBDM translates images into corresponding photo-realistic images while being conditioned solely on the given exemplar image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EBDM formulates the image translation task as a stochastic Brownian bridge process, allowing for translation into photo-realistic images conditioned solely on the given exemplar image.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly combines the formulation and the translation result; the translation conditioned on an exemplar is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The incorporation of the Global Encoder, Exemplar Network, and Exemplar Attention Module allows for the efficient guidance of the diffusion process toward the style of the exemplar.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of the Global Encoder, Exemplar Network, and Exemplar Attention Module enables the network to effectively utilize global and detailed texture information from exemplar images.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution in terms of effective utilization of global and detailed texture information, clarifying the mechanism and benefit of those modules.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network's use of Bridge diffusion leads to more robust training and inference processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EBDM leads to more robust training and inference processes compared to previous methodologies that rely on dense correspondences.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim by explicitly comparing robustness to prior dense-correspondence methods, amplifying the original assertion of improved robustness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The superiority of EBDM over competing approaches is demonstrated through comprehensive benchmark evaluations and visual results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comprehensive benchmark evaluations demonstrate the superiority of EBDM over competing approaches in image translation tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the benchmark-based superiority claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HPO algorithms demonstrate significant runtime advantages over Random Search. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HPO algorithms demonstrate significant runtime advantages over Random Search. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in wording and meaning to the preprint claim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In univariate models, Bayesian optimization exhibited the lowest accuracy among the tested methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In univariate models, Bayesian optimization exhibited the lowest accuracy among the tested methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is repeated verbatim with the same scope and confidence in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides valuable insights for optimizing XGBoost in the STLF context. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study offers valuable insights for optimizing XGBoost in the context of STLF. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution with equivalent meaning (minor wording change only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of five HPO algorithms was investigated across univariate and multivariate STLF tasks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of HPO algorithms was evaluated using accuracy metrics such as MAPE and R2. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from the breadth of tasks (uni-/multivariate comparison across five algorithms) to the evaluation metrics used (MAPE, R2), narrowing and clarifying the reported evaluation focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated high predictive accuracy, outperforming conventional statistical methods in terms of both speed and reliability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrates significant improvements in speed and reliability over conventional image processing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the performance claim but changes the comparator from "conventional statistical methods" to "conventional image processing methods," representing a revision and a narrower/shifted comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The artificial neural network framework can serve as an effective tool for real-time monitoring and management of water pollution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial neural networks can serve as an effective tool for real-time monitoring and management of water pollution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the conclusion with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach enhances our understanding of pollution dynamics and underscores the potential of machine learning techniques in environmental science. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or restate this broader contribution about enhancing understanding and potential of ML, so the preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional neural network trained under a transfer learning strategy with AlexNet achieves a high validation accuracy of 99.85% and a precision of 99.85%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a detailed result specifying CNN architecture and exact metrics that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Agents can learn a fair assignment of goals while achieving almost perfect goal coverage in navigation scenarios using only local observations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Agents can learn to be fair without significantly sacrificing efficiency when trained using min-max fair distance goal assignments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core result about learning fair goal assignments with preserved efficiency; wording differs slightly but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model yields a 14% improvement in efficiency and a 5% improvement in fairness over a baseline trained using random assignments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model yields a 14% improvement in efficiency and a 5% improvement in fairness over a baseline model trained using random assignments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric improvements and comparison are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An average of 21% improvement in fairness can be achieved compared to a model trained on optimally efficient assignments, with only a 7% decrease in efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An average of 21% improvement in fairness can be achieved by the proposed model compared to a model trained on optimally efficient assignments, with only a 7% decrease in efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the quantitative trade-off claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to complete coverage tasks in prescribed formations without tailoring the models to specific formation shapes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to complete coverage tasks in prescribed formations without tailoring the models to specific formation shapes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about formation-general coverage is retained unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ReeFRAME is a scalable Reeb graph-based framework designed to analyze vast volumes of GPS-enabled human trajectory data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ReeFRAME is a scalable Reeb graph-based framework designed to analyze vast volumes of GPS-enabled human trajectory data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ReeFRAME models Patterns-of-life (PoL) at both the population and individual levels using Multi-Agent Reeb Graphs (MARGs) and Temporal Reeb Graphs (TERGs). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ReeFRAME models Patterns-of-life (PoL) at both the population and individual levels using Multi-Agent Reeb Graphs (MARGs) and Temporal Reeb Graphs (TERGs). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical description of modeling approach and scope is present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework's linear algorithmic complexity relative to the number of time points ensures scalability for anomaly detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework's linear algorithmic complexity relative to the number of time points ensures scalability for anomaly detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The algorithmic complexity and its implication for scalability are stated the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ReeFRAME has been validated on six large-scale anomaly detection datasets simulating real-time patterns with up to 500,000 agents over two months. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ReeFRAME has been validated on six large-scale anomaly detection datasets simulating real-time patterns with up to 500,000 agents over two months. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validation dataset description and results are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The utilisation of a small, correctly annotated validation set is instrumental in avoiding overfitting and maintaining model performance to a large extent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The utilisation of a small, correctly annotated validation set is instrumental in avoiding overfitting and maintaining model performance to a large extent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings underscore the beneficial role of pre-training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings underscore the beneficial role of pre-training. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same point about pre-training benefits with equivalent emphasis and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Noisy annotations negatively impact the training and performance of a CNN model for detecting, segmenting, and classifying nuclei in histopathology images. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Noisy annotations negatively impact the training and performance of a state-of-the-art CNN model for detecting, segmenting, and classifying nuclei in histopathology images. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the model as "state-of-the-art," adding specificity to the original claim without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Jeffreys-Fisher-Rao center serves as a plug-in replacement for the Jeffreys centroid and provides a closed-form formula for categorical and normal distributions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Jeffreys-Fisher-Rao center provides a plug-in replacement for the Jeffreys centroid and has a closed-form formula for categorical and multivariate normal distributions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution but specifies 'multivariate normal' (a clarification of the original "normal" wording) while keeping the same role and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Jeffreys-Fisher-Rao center matches exactly the Jeffreys centroid for same-mean normal distributions and is experimentally observed to be close to the Jeffreys centroid.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Jeffreys-Fisher-Rao center matches exactly the Jeffreys centroid for same-mean normal distributions and is experimentally observed to be close to the Jeffreys centroid.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Gauss-Bregman inductive center approximates the Jeffreys centroid very well and converges for sets of same-mean normal distributions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Gauss-Bregman double sequences converge and match the Jeffreys centroid for sets of same-mean normal distributions.
alignments.rationale: The publication more explicitly states convergence and matching to the Jeffreys centroid (stronger language and higher certainty) while preserving the original scope (same-mean normals).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new type of inductive centers generalizes the principle of Gauss arithmetic-geometric double sequence mean for pairs of densities of any given exponential family.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to this generalization; the published paper does not appear to include this claimed broader theoretical generalization.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Gauss-Bregman center approximates the Jeffreys centroid very well and is suggested as a replacement when the Jeffreys-Fisher-Rao center is not available in closed form.
alignments.rationale: This explicit recommendation (using Gauss-Bregman as a fallback) is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The closed-form formula of the Jeffreys-Fisher-Rao center for categorical distributions approximates the numerical Jeffreys centroid effectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit result about how the closed-form categorical JFR formula approximates the numerical centroid, which was not separately claimed in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proportion of weights that can be pruned without degrading performance is largely invariant to model size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proportion of weights that can be pruned without degrading performance is largely invariant to model size.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increasing the width of a model has little effect on the density of the pruned model relative to the increase in absolute size of the pruned network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increasing the width of a model has little effect on the density of the pruned model relative to the increase in absolute size of the pruned network.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is substantial prunability across a large range of model sizes, with the largest model being 50 times as wide as the smallest model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is substantial prunability across a large range of model sizes, with the largest model being 50 times as wide as the smallest model.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this empirical finding without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Retinal optical coherence tomography (OCT) imaging combined with fundus photographs can identify future adverse cardiac events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of retinal optical coherence tomography (OCT) imaging in conjunction with fundus photographs for identifying future adverse cardiac events.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the finding as demonstrating potential (more cautious phrasing) rather than the stronger definitive phrasing in the preprint; scope remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Multi-channel Variational Autoencoder (MCVAE) model classifies individuals into groups based on their likelihood of developing cardiovascular diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model, trained on both imaging modalities, achieved promising results (AUROC 0.78 ± 0.02, accuracy 0.68 ± 0.002, precision 0.74 ± 0.02, sensitivity 0.73 ± 0.02, and specificity 0.68 ± 0.01), demonstrating its efficacy in identifying patients at risk of future CVD events based on their retinal images.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the same contribution and ties it directly to quantitative performance, clarifying model evaluation but not changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved an AUROC of 0.78, accuracy of 0.68, precision of 0.74, sensitivity of 0.73, and specificity of 0.68, demonstrating its efficacy in identifying patients at risk of future CVD events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model, trained on both imaging modalities, achieved promising results (AUROC 0.78 ± 0.02, accuracy 0.68 ± 0.002, precision 0.74 ± 0.02, sensitivity 0.73 ± 0.02, and specificity 0.68 ± 0.01), demonstrating its efficacy in identifying patients at risk of future CVD events based on their retinal images.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same numerical performance with added uncertainty estimates, which clarifies but does not materially change the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Retinal OCT imaging and fundus photographs are cost-effective, non-invasive alternatives for predicting cardiovascular disease risk.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study highlights the potential of retinal OCT imaging and fundus photographs as cost-effective, non-invasive alternatives for predicting cardiovascular disease risk.
alignments.rationale: The publication couches this as a potential (slightly more cautious wording) but preserves the same scope and confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings contribute to the development of standardized methods for early cardiovascular disease risk identification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings contribute to the development of standardized, accessible methods for early CVD risk identification, potentially improving preventive care strategies and patient outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the original contribution by emphasizing accessibility and potential impact on preventive care, broadening the practical implications while keeping the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The syren-new emulator achieves root mean squared errors of 0.1%, 0.3%, and 1.3% for the derived parameter sigma_8, the linear power spectrum, and the nonlinear power spectrum, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analytic emulators for σ 8, and the linear and nonlinear power spectra achieve root mean squared errors of 0.1%, 0.3%, and 1.3%, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same numerical emulator accuracies with equivalent phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The emulator-related discrepancies are subdominant compared to observational errors and other modeling uncertainties when computing shear power spectra for LSST-like surveys.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Emulator-related discrepancies are subdominant compared to observational errors and other modeling uncertainties when computing shear power spectra for LSST-like surveys.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion but marks it with somewhat lower emphasis/certainty (publication labels it medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The syren-new emulator provides similar accuracy to existing numerical emulators but is at least an order of magnitude faster on both CPU and GPU.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The symbolic approximations now cover the same range of cosmological models as many numerical emulators with similar accuracy, but are much faster and more interpretable.
alignments.rationale: The publication elaborates on applicability and interpretability while preserving the speed/accuracy comparison; it broadens the claimed applicability and clarifies interpretability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work improves the accuracy, speed, and range of applicability of current symbolic approximations to the linear and nonlinear matter power spectra.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work greatly improves the accuracy, speed, and applicability range of current symbolic approximations of the linear and nonlinear matter power spectra.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens and slightly broadens the original contribution by emphasizing 'greatly' and noting parity with numerical emulators over a wider model range.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The error on the nonlinear power spectrum is reduced by approximately a factor of 2 when considering observationally plausible dark energy models and neutrino masses.
alignments.rationale: This specific quantitative improvement under additional cosmological variations appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Contrastive pre-training on medical images has strong potential.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate the broad claim of 'strong potential'; instead it focuses on dataset reduction findings and dataset quality, so this general claim was de-emphasized/omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The similarity of medical images hinders the success of contrastive learning in the medical imaging domain.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly alleges that image similarity hinders contrastive learning; the published work instead emphasizes dataset reduction benefits and quality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dataset reduction leads to a considerable performance gain in downstream tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dataset reduction leads to a considerable performance gain in downstream tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this empirical finding directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pre-training is up to nine times faster due to the dataset reduction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pre-training is up to nine times faster due to the dataset reduction.
alignments.rationale: The speedup claim is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach highlights the importance of dataset quality for improving contrastive pre-training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach highlights the importance of dataset quality for contrastive pre-training in medical images.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward this conclusion with the same emphasis and scope (medical images).

alignments.pub_claim_text: The AUC score improves from 0.78 to 0.83 for the COVID CT Classification Grand Challenge due to dataset reduction.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific numerical example of downstream AUC improvement that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method provides a theoretical guarantee of convergence in terms of movement costs for Bayesian Optimization. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides a theoretical guarantee of convergence in terms of movement costs for Bayesian Optimization. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact theoretical guarantee claim with the same wording and certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirically, our method effectively reduces average movement costs over time while maintaining comparable regret performance to conventional Bayesian Optimization methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirically, our method effectively reduces average movement costs over time while maintaining comparable regret performance to conventional Bayesian Optimization methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical result is restated identically in the publication with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This framework shows promise for broader applications in various bandit settings with movement costs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This framework shows promise for broader applications in various bandit settings with movement costs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved verbatim and same certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NeUClust achieves $ \tilde{O}(\tilde{d}\,\text{sqrt}(T))$ regret in the contextual combinatorial bandit setting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NeUClust achieves sublinear regret in the number of rounds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication weakens the specific rate to a more general 'sublinear regret' statement, removing the precise bound; this is a revision that broadens the claim scope and reduces specificity/confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NeUClust uses a neural network to estimate the super arm reward and select the super arm, eliminating the need for a known optimization oracle. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NeUClust uses a neural network to estimate the super arm reward and select the super arm, thus eliminating the need for a known optimization oracle. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim text is effectively the same, but the publication reports lower certainty (medium), so the claim content is unchanged while the expressed confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on real world recommendation datasets show that NeUClust achieves better regret and reward than other contextual combinatorial and neural bandit algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NeUClust achieves better regret and reward than other contextual combinatorial and neural bandit algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical comparative result is restated with the same meaning and certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work presents a new paradigm to automated validation of anomaly-detection models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work presents a new paradigm to automated validation of anomaly-detection models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim verbatim and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach is inspired by real-world, collaborative decision-making mechanisms. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach is inspired by real-world, collaborative decision-making mechanisms. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The inspiration and framing are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experimental results demonstrate the accuracy and robustness of our approach on model selection and model evaluation tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experimental results demonstrate the accuracy and robustness of our approach on model selection and model evaluation tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental results claim is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Procedural Content Generation (PCG) can increase player engagement and ease the work of game designers. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Procedural Content Generation (PCG) can increase player engagement and ease the work of game designers. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about PCG's benefits is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The arrival of Large Language Models (LLMs) has disrupted the trajectory of PCG advancement. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The arrival of Large Language Models (LLMs) has disrupted the trajectory of PCG advancement. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This survey explores the differences between various algorithms used for PCG, including search-based methods, machine learning-based methods, and LLMs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This survey explores the differences between various algorithms used for PCG, including search-based methods, machine learning-based methods, and LLMs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The scope and content of the survey are stated the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify gaps in the existing academic work on PCG and suggest possible directions for future research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify gaps in the existing academic work and suggest possible directions for future research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the identification of gaps and future directions with essentially identical meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heterogeneous Sequential Feature Forest Flow (HS3F) generates data sequentially, reducing the dependency on noisy initial conditions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Heterogeneous Sequential Feature Forest Flow (HS3F) generates data sequentially, reducing the dependency on noisy initial conditions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HS3F produces higher quality and more diverse synthetic data than the Forest Flow (FF) method, especially for categorical variables. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HS3F produces higher quality and more diverse synthetic data than the Forest Flow (FF) method, especially for categorical variables. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical result about quality and diversity is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HS3F generates data 21-27 times faster for datasets with 20% or more categorical variables compared to FF. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HS3F generates data 21-27 times faster for datasets with 20% or more categorical variables compared to FF. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported speedup is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HS3F demonstrates enhanced robustness to affine transformation in flow ODE initial conditions compared to FF. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HS3F demonstrates enhanced robustness to affine transformation in flow ODE initial conditions compared to FF. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The robustness claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SLNN and MLNN decoders can always achieve maximum likelihood performance, regardless of the code dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SLNN and MLNN decoders can always achieve maximum likelihood performance, regardless of the code dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this general constraint about FWI starting models; it focuses on algorithmic and uncertainty-estimation contributions rather than this methodological caveat.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian inference offers an alternative approach to quantify the uncertainty associated with the recovered solution in FWI.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Technological applications impose a variety selection bias that affects linguistic diversity. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Línguas dominantes e bem documentadas são preservadas e padronizadas por fornecerem os dados necessários para o treinamento de modelos de língua em larga escala. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel method MamT$^4$ enables simultaneous analysis of four mammography images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study achieves a ROC-AUC of 84.0 ± 1.7 on an independent test dataset of Vietnam digital mammography. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method also achieves an F1 score of 56.0 ± 1.3 on the same independent test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method achieves an F1 score of $56.0 \times 1.3$ on the independent test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same F1 result (formatting differences aside) with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The preprocessing of images using a cropping model helps remove image artifacts and focus on the breast region. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework leverages event modality to guide the learning of a unified illumination-invariant representation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed SambaMixer model outperforms the state-of-the-art in predicting the state of health of Li-ion batteries. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed omics-driven modeling pipeline integrates machine-learning tools to facilitate the dynamic modeling of multiscale biological systems.
